perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Confidential Information" means non-public information that a Party or Its affiliates ("disclosing Party") discloses to the other Party or its affiliates ("receiving Party") which is designated as being 'proprietary' or 'confidential' or which by its nature or the circumstances reasonably ought to be treated as confidential. Confidential Information includes the disclosing Party's software end prototypes and information relating to the disclosing Party's business affairs, including business methods, marketing strategies, pricing, competitor Information, product development strategies, and financial results. Confidential Information does not include information which (a) is known by the receiving Party, free of any obligation to keep it confidential; (b) is at the time of disclosure, or thereafter becomes, publicly available through no wrongful act of the receiving Party; (c) Is independently developed by the receiving Party, without relying on or referring to the Confidential Information of disclosing Party; or (d) is approved for release by prior written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Confidential Information" means non-public information that a Party or Its affiliates ("disclosing Party") discloses to the other Party or its affiliates ("receiving Party"). Confidential Information includes only the disclosing Party's software and prototypes. Confidential Information does not include information which (a) is known by the receiving Party, free of any obligation to keep it confidential; (b) is at the time of disclosure, or thereafter becomes, publicly available through no wrongful act of the receiving Party; (c) Is independently developed by the receiving Party, without relying on or referring to the Confidential Information of disclosing Party; or (d) is approved for release by prior written authorization of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly narrows the definition of "Confidential Information," removing the phrase "which is designated as being 'proprietary' or 'confidential' or which by its nature or the circumstances reasonably ought to be treated as confidential" and restricting it to only the disclosing party's software and prototypes. This contradicts Section 4.7, where 'all data, information relating to Services, including but not limit to the customer personal data' is treated as confidential. This creates ambiguity as customer personal data and other sensitive business data are not explicitly covered under the narrowed definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.7 Data protection. Each Party agrees to comply with all applicable data protection and privacy laws arising from its obligations, if any, under the Agreement and to reasonably co-operate with the other Party in order to allow the other Party to comply with any laws as deemed necessary from time to time. Each Party shall treat all data, information relating to Services, including but not limit to the customer personal data, as confidential. Each party warrants that it shall take appropriate technical and organizational measures against unauthorized or unlawful processing of personal data and against accidental loss or destruction of, or damage to, the personal data of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of "Confidential Information" to only include "the disclosing Party's software and prototypes." This directly contradicts Section 4.7, which states, "Each Party shall treat all data, information relating to Services, including but not limit to the customer personal data, as confidential." The narrowed definition excludes customer personal data and other business data from being automatically considered 'Confidential Information', creating a clear and impactful contradiction regarding the scope of confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue in effect for the Service Period, unless terminated earlier as provided in this Section. This Agreement is a memorialization of an earlier oral Agreement and shall be deemed to apply for the entire Service Period. The Agreement may be extended or renewed by the Parties in a writing executed by authorized representatives of each of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 The Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue in effect for the Service Period, unless terminated earlier as provided in this Section. The Agreement may be terminated by either party at will, with no cause needed, with a one-day written notice. This Agreement is a memorialization of an earlier oral Agreement and shall be deemed to apply for the entire Service Period. The Agreement may be extended or renewed by the Parties in a writing executed by authorized representatives of each of the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change inserts a clause allowing either party to terminate the agreement 'at will, with no cause needed, with a one-day written notice'. This directly contradicts Section 9.2, which specifies that the agreement can only be terminated prior to the end of the Service Period if the other party is in material breach and fails to remedy it within 30 days. This creates a significant conflict regarding the termination terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.2 This Agreement may be terminated by either Party prior to the end of the Service Period if the other Party is in material breach of any tem, or condition of this Agreement and such breach la not remedied for a period of thirty (30) days after the Party in breach has been notified In writing of such breach by the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing termination at will with one day's notice. This directly contradicts Section 9.2, which states that termination before the end of the Service Period is only allowed if the other party is in material breach and fails to remedy it within 30 days. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the termination terms, thus making the contract unenforceable as to termination.
